perturbation.original_text: Term . The term of this Agreement commences on the Effective Date and ends on the date one (1) year thereafter. Receiving Party’s obligations to protect Confidential Information disclosed under this Agreement shall survive termination of this Agreement and will be binding upon Receiving Party, its heirs, successors, and assigns for a period of five (5) years from expiration or termination of this Agreement.
perturbation.changed_text: Term . The term of this Agreement commences on the Effective Date and ends on the date one (1) year thereafter. Receiving Party’s obligations to protect Confidential Information disclosed under this Agreement shall terminate immediately upon expiration or termination of this Agreement.
perturbation.explanation: The original text extends the confidentiality obligations for five years after the agreement's termination. The modified text removes this extended obligation, stating that confidentiality obligations end immediately upon termination or expiration. This directly contradicts Section 4(f) regarding the return of materials, which implies an ongoing obligation to protect Confidential Information even after termination. (f) Return of Materials . Immediately upon (a) termination or expiration of this Agreement, (b) the decision by either Party not to enter into the business or scientific relationship contemplated above, or (c) a request by Disclosing Party at any time, Receiving Party will promptly turn over to Disclosing Party, or destroy, all Confidential Information of Disclosing Party and all documents, media, and other tangible materials containing any such Confidential Information and any and all extracts thereof.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Immediately upon (a) termination or expiration of this Agreement, (b) the decision by either Party not to enter into the business or scientific relationship contemplated above, or (c) a request by Disclosing Party at any time, Receiving Party will promptly turn over to Disclosing Party, or destroy, all Confidential Information of Disclosing Party and all documents, media, and other tangible materials containing any such Confidential Information and any and all extracts thereof. In the event that Receiving Party destroys Confidential Information, upon the destruction thereof, Receiving Party will issue to Disclosing Party a certificate as proof of compliance with Disclosing Party’s request. Notwithstanding this Section 4(f) , the Receiving Party shall not be required to purge Confidential Information from its computer system’s historical back-up media, provided that such Confidential Information that is retained will remain subject to the terms of this Agreement.
perturbation.justification: The original text states that confidentiality obligations survive for five years after termination. The changed text states that these obligations terminate immediately upon termination. This creates a direct contradiction with the contradicted text, Section 4(f), which requires the Receiving Party to return or destroy Confidential Information upon termination, implying an ongoing obligation to protect Confidential Information even after termination. The changed text therefore removes a key obligation that Section 4(f) relies upon, thus creating a strong contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: Use; Disclosure . Receiving Party shall use the Confidential Information solely for the Purpose defined above. Receiving Party shall not use the Confidential Information for any other purpose, including but not limited to using it in connection with the development or commercialization of any process or product, or using it in connection with any submission to any governmental agency, including any patent office or regulatory authority, or the like, without the express written permission of Disclosing Party.
perturbation.changed_text: Use; Disclosure . Receiving Party may use the Confidential Information for any purpose it deems necessary. Receiving Party shall not be restricted in the use of confidential information.
perturbation.explanation: This change completely reverses the original intention. The original text restricted the use of Confidential Information to the defined 'Purpose'. The modified text removes this restriction, allowing the Receiving Party to use the information for any purpose. This contradicts Section 4(g), which states that the agreement does not grant any right to Receiving Party under any patent, copyright or other intellectual property right of Disclosing Party. (g) No Transfer or License . Nothing in this Agreement is intended to grant or transfer any right to Receiving Party under any patent, copyright or other intellectual property right of Disclosing Party, nor shall this Agreement grant or transfer to the Receiving Party any right in or to the Confidential Information except as expressly set forth herein.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Nothing in this Agreement is intended to grant or transfer any right to Receiving Party under any patent, copyright or other intellectual property right of Disclosing Party, nor shall this Agreement grant or transfer to the Receiving Party any right in or to the Confidential Information except as expressly set forth herein. None of the Confidential Information which may be disclosed by Disclosing Party shall constitute any representation, warranty, assurance, guarantee or inducement by Disclosing Party to Receiving Party, including, without limitation, with respect to the non-infringement of intellectual property rights, or other rights of third persons.
perturbation.justification: The changed text allows the Receiving Party to use confidential information for any purpose, effectively granting them broad rights. This directly contradicts the contradicted text, which explicitly states that the agreement does not grant any right to the Receiving Party under any patent, copyright, or other intellectual property right of the Disclosing Party and that no rights to the confidential information are granted except as expressly set forth. The original text restricted the use to the 'Purpose defined above' and this change removes those restrictions. This creates a direct and undeniable conflict, hence 'YES'.

perturbation.original_text: Receiving Party shall hold the Confidential Information in strict confidence, and shall take all reasonable precautions to protect the Confidential Information at all times from unauthorized disclosure, publication, or use, including, without limitation, using at least the same degree of care as it employs to protect its own Confidential Information of like nature (but in any event no less than a reasonable degree of care), acting in a manner consistent with its obligations under this Agreement.
perturbation.changed_text: Receiving Party shall make reasonable efforts to protect confidential information. Receiving party is responsible for their own degree of care.
perturbation.explanation: The original clause mandates 'strict confidence' and 'all reasonable precautions', establishing a high standard of care. The revised text replaces this with 'reasonable efforts', significantly lowering the required standard. This creates a contradiction with Section 4(d) Legally Required Disclosures. Nothing in this Agreement shall preclude Receiving Party from making any disclosure of Confidential Information that is required by applicable law or regulation or by a valid order of a court or other governmental body having jurisdiction, provided that Receiving Party uses best efforts to limit the scope of the required disclosure
perturbation.contradicted_text: Nothing in this Agreement shall preclude Receiving Party from making any disclosure of Confidential Information that is required by applicable law or regulation or by a valid order of a court or other governmental body having jurisdiction, provided that Receiving Party uses best efforts to limit the scope of the required disclosure, provides notification to Disclosing Party of such requirement as soon as reasonably possible, and cooperates with Disclosing Party in seeking an appropriate protective order, confidential treatment, or similar remedy limiting the subsequent use and disclosure of any information required to be disclosed.
perturbation.justification: The original text requires 'strict confidence' and 'all reasonable precautions'. The changed text lowers this to 'reasonable efforts'. This contradicts the 'best efforts' requirement in the contradicted text regarding legally required disclosures, creating an in-text contradiction. The explanation supports this conclusion.

perturbation.original_text: Nothing in this Agreement is intended to grant or transfer any right to Receiving Party under any patent, copyright or other intellectual property right of Disclosing Party, nor shall this Agreement grant or transfer to the Receiving Party any right in or to the Confidential Information except as expressly set forth herein.
perturbation.changed_text: Receiving Party shall have the right to utilize Confidential Information for their own proprietary development purposes.
perturbation.explanation: The original clause explicitly states that the agreement does not grant any intellectual property rights or rights to the Confidential Information. The modified text directly contradicts this, granting the Receiving Party the right to use the information for their own development purposes. This new right conflicts with Section 5(a), which restricts the use of names and publicity related to the Disclosing Party. Using the information for proprietary development could indirectly lead to unauthorized use of the Disclosing Party's name or association with their products. (a) Use of Names; Publicity . Except as otherwise provided herein, nothing contained in this Agreement shall be construed as conferring any right on Receiving Party to use in any manner Disclosing Party’s name or any trade name or trademark.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Except as otherwise provided herein, nothing contained in this Agreement shall be construed as conferring any right on Receiving Party to use in any manner Disclosing Party’s name or any trade name or trademark. Receiving Party will make no public announcement or other public statement concerning the existence of this Agreement or the Parties’ respective performances hereunder without the prior written consent of Disclosing Party, which may be withheld in Disclosing Party’s sole and absolute discretion, except as necessary to comply with applicable law or regulations.
perturbation.justification: The original text explicitly denies the transfer of intellectual property rights to the Receiving Party. The changed text grants the Receiving Party the right to use Confidential Information for their own proprietary development purposes. This creates a direct contradiction because using the information for proprietary development directly conflicts with the contradicted text, which prohibits the Receiving Party from using the Disclosing Party's name or trademarks without prior written consent.

perturbation.original_text: Receiving Party agrees that disclosure of Confidential Information without the express written permission of Disclosing Party will cause Disclosing Party irreparable harm and that any breach or threatened breach of this Agreement by Receiving Party will entitle Disclosing Party to injunctive relief, in addition to any other legal and/or equitable remedies available to it. Notwithstanding clause (f) , Disclosing Party may seek injunctive relief in any court of competent jurisdiction.
perturbation.changed_text: Disclosing party's sole remedy for any breach of this agreement is monetary damages.
perturbation.explanation: The original text states that the Disclosing Party is entitled to injunctive relief for any breach involving the disclosure of Confidential Information. The modified text limits the Disclosing Party's remedy to monetary damages, explicitly removing the right to seek injunctive relief. This contradicts Section 5(c), which states that if any provision of the agreement is held invalid, the remaining provisions shall remain in effect. Eliminating injunctive relief affects a core provision protecting Confidential Information, and its removal fundamentally alters the agreement's balance, making consistent enforcement difficult and affecting how the rest of the agreement is interpreted. (c) Severability . If any provision of this Agreement should be held invalid or unenforceable, the remaining provisions shall be unaffected and shall remain in full force and effect, to the extent consistent with the intent of the parties as evidenced by this Agreement as a whole.
perturbation.contradicted_text: If any provision of this Agreement should be held invalid or unenforceable, the remaining provisions shall be unaffected and shall remain in full force and effect, to the extent consistent with the intent of the parties as evidenced by this Agreement as a whole.
perturbation.justification: The original text allows the Disclosing Party to seek injunctive relief for breaches, especially those involving Confidential Information. The changed text restricts the Disclosing Party's remedy to only monetary damages, removing the possibility of injunctive relief. This directly contradicts the contradicted text, which states that if any provision is unenforceable, the remaining provisions remain in effect consistent with the intent of the parties. Eliminating injunctive relief, a core provision for protecting Confidential Information, fundamentally alters the agreement and its intent, creating a direct conflict.
